fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 70, `if not clamp:`, is inverted. It should be `if clamp:`, causing the clamping logic to be applied when disabled and skipped when enabled.

constraints.intent: When the interpolated value is within the y-range, the bug has no effect, so the program behaves correctly.
If clamping is enabled (which leads to this branch), the interpolated value must already be within the valid range for the program to be correct.
The clamping logic (the 'then' branch) should only execute when clamping is enabled.
